t: AI Coding — Tools, Agents & LLM APIs for Engineers
d: AI coding deep dives — Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code, prompt engineering, AI agents, building with LLM APIs, tool use & MCP, and AI in interviews.

t: AI-Assisted Coding: Best Practices & Pitfalls
d: How to use AI coding tools without losing control of your codebase — reviewing every diff, maintaining understanding, managing secrets and compliance, avoiding technical debt, and measuring real productivity.

t: Building with the Claude & OpenAI APIs
d: A comprehensive guide to building with the Claude and OpenAI APIs — Messages API, streaming, prompt caching, token costs, latency optimization, structured output, and security.

t: Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code — AI Coding Tools Compared
d: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code compared — how each AI coding tool works, their strengths and weaknesses, and when to use which.

t: Harness Engineering — Building Agents Around LLMs
d: Harness engineering — the discipline of building the scaffolding around an LLM that turns a text model into a capable agent: the agent loop, context assembly, tool design, memory, guardrails, verification, and evaluation.

t: How AI Coding Agents Work
d: A deep dive into how AI coding agents work — the agentic loop, tool use, ReAct pattern, context management, task planning, and self-verification.

t: Prompt Engineering for Code
d: Why the quality of your prompt determines the quality of the code — context, constraints, few-shot examples, planning-first, test-driven prompting, and the anti-patterns that silently kill output quality.

t: SDE Interviews in the AI Era
d: When AI can write boilerplate, what do SDE interviews actually test? A deep dive into the durable skills that matter more than ever: system design, debugging, code review, algorithmic intuition, and working with AI fluently.

t: Tool Use, Function Calling & MCP
d: A deep dive into function calling, tool use, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — how models call tools, JSON schema definitions, parallel calls, and why MCP matters for agent ecosystems.

t: Using AI in Coding Interviews
d: When can you use AI in a coding interview? A deep dive into where AI tools are banned, where they're allowed, how to prepare smarter with AI, and how to talk about it honestly with interviewers.

t: Contact & Mock Interview — updoggtech
d: Book a 1:1 mock interview or consultation for SDE interviews. System design and coding interview prep.

t: DDIA Ch.1 — Reliable, Scalable & Maintainable
d: Ch.1 of DDIA: what makes a system reliable, how to reason about scalability with load parameters and percentile latency, and the three pillars of maintainability.

t: DDIA Ch.10 — Batch Processing
d: Ch.10 of DDIA: batch processing — the Unix philosophy, MapReduce and distributed filesystems, joins and grouping, dataflow engines like Spark and Flink, and the output of batch workflows.

t: DDIA Ch.11 — Stream Processing
d: Ch.11 of DDIA: stream processing — event streams, message brokers, log-based vs traditional queues, change data capture, event sourcing, reasoning about time and windows, stream joins, and fault tolerance.

t: DDIA Ch.12 — The Future of Data Systems
d: Ch.12 of DDIA: the future of data systems — data integration via derived data, unbundling the database, dataflow and materialized views, end-to-end correctness, and the ethics of data.

t: DDIA Ch.2 — Data Models & Query Languages
d: Ch.2 of DDIA: relational vs document vs graph data models, impedance mismatch, schema-on-read vs schema-on-write, and declarative vs imperative query languages.

t: DDIA Ch.3 — Storage & Retrieval
d: Ch.3 of DDIA: how databases store and retrieve data — log-structured storage, SSTable, LSM-tree, B-tree, secondary indexes, column stores, and OLTP vs OLAP.

t: DDIA Ch.4 — Encoding & Evolution
d: Ch.4 of DDIA: how data is encoded for storage and transmission, backward vs forward compatibility, schema evolution with Protocol Buffers, Thrift, and Avro, and the three modes of dataflow — databases, services, and message passing.

t: DDIA Ch.5 — Replication
d: Ch.5 of DDIA: replication strategies — single-leader, multi-leader, and leaderless — synchronous vs asynchronous, failover dangers, replication lag anomalies, and quorum reads/writes.

t: DDIA Ch.6 — Partitioning
d: Ch.6 of DDIA: partitioning (sharding) — key-range vs hash partitioning, hot spots and skew, partitioning secondary indexes by document vs term, rebalancing, and request routing.

t: DDIA Ch.7 — Transactions
d: Ch.7 of DDIA: transactions and ACID, weak isolation levels (read committed, snapshot isolation/MVCC), lost updates, write skew and phantoms, and serializability via serial execution, two-phase locking, and SSI.

t: DDIA Ch.8 — The Trouble with Distributed Systems
d: Ch.8 of DDIA: the trouble with distributed systems — partial failure, unreliable networks and timeouts, unreliable clocks and process pauses, fencing tokens, Byzantine faults, and system models.

t: DDIA Ch.9 — Consistency & Consensus
d: Ch.9 of DDIA: consistency and consensus — linearizability, the CAP theorem, ordering and causality, Lamport timestamps, total order broadcast, two-phase commit, consensus algorithms, and ZooKeeper.

t: DDIA Notes — Designing Data-Intensive Applications — updoggtech
d: Condensed notes from Designing Data-Intensive Applications (DDIA) — reliability, data models, storage engines, and more.

t: Home
d: updoggtech — software interview prep. System design, tech stacks, and DDIA notes for SDE interviews.

t: Design Google Drive / Dropbox — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building Google Drive / Dropbox — file chunking, content-addressed deduplication, splitting metadata from block storage, the sync algorithm, change notifications, and conflict resolution.

t: Design Nearby / Yelp (Proximity Service) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a proximity / nearby-places service (Yelp) — geospatial indexing with geohash and quadtrees, the boundary problem, read-heavy caching, ranking, and sharding by region.

t: Design Pastebin — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into Pastebin — key generation, separating metadata from blob storage, read-heavy caching and CDN, expiration and cleanup, and abuse prevention.

t: Design Search Autocomplete (Typeahead) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into search autocomplete (typeahead) — the trie data structure, precomputed top-k suggestions, ranking, the offline data pipeline, sharding, caching, and real-time vs batch updates.

t: Design Ticketmaster — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into Ticketmaster — preventing double-booking with seat holds and locking, hold expiration, the virtual waiting room for onsale spikes, strong consistency for inventory, and payment integration.

t: Design a Chat App (WhatsApp) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a chat app at scale — WebSocket connection management, Snowflake IDs, offline delivery, and group messaging architecture.

t: Design a Distributed Key-Value Store — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a distributed key-value store (Dynamo-style) — consistent hashing, replication and quorums, tunable consistency, vector clocks, hinted handoff, Merkle-tree anti-entropy, and gossip membership.

t: Design a Hotel Booking System
d: A system design deep dive into building a hotel booking app — room availability tracking, oversell prevention with Redis TTL, ACID booking records, and Elasticsearch-powered search.

t: Design a Leaderboard — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a real-time leaderboard — why SQL ORDER BY doesn't scale, Redis sorted sets for O(log n) rank queries, persistence, sharding, and exact vs approximate global rank.

t: Design a Map Web App (Google Maps) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a map web app at scale — geospatial indexing, route pre-computation, WebSocket navigation, and ML-powered ETA refinement.

t: Design a Payment System — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into a payment system — the payment flow through a PSP, idempotency to prevent double charges, the double-entry ledger, exactly-once via reconciliation, sagas, and PCI compliance.

t: Design a Ride-Sharing App (Uber) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a ride-share app — GPS ingestion, bipartite graph matching, geospatial indexing, and async payment processing.

t: Design a URL Shortener (TinyURL) — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a URL shortener — hashing vs. ID generation, Bloom filter collision detection, TTL purging strategies, and rate limiting.

t: Design a Video Streaming Platform (Netflix/YouTube)
d: A system design deep dive into building a video streaming platform covering upload pipelines, transcoding, CDN delivery, and ML-powered recommendations.

t: Design an E-commerce Platform — System Design
d: A system design deep dive into building a scalable e-commerce platform covering microservices, inventory, search, and event-driven order processing.

t: System Design Interview Questions & Breakdowns — updoggtech
d: System design interview prep — e-commerce, video streaming, chat app, ride-share, URL shortener and more.

t: API Gateway & Service Mesh
d: A deep dive into API gateways and service meshes — handling cross-cutting concerns in microservices, north-south vs east-west traffic, the BFF pattern, the sidecar proxy model, mTLS, and gateway vs mesh.

t: Apache Kafka — Pub/Sub, Partitions & Exactly-Once
d: A deep dive into Apache Kafka — pub/sub model, producers, consumers, partitions, replicas, acks, exactly-once delivery, and ZooKeeper.

t: CDN — Content Delivery Networks
d: A deep dive into CDNs — why edge caching cuts latency and offloads the origin, how PoPs and anycast routing work, cache-control and TTLs, push vs pull, invalidation strategies, and dynamic acceleration.

t: CI/CD — Continuous Integration & Delivery
d: A deep dive into CI/CD — continuous integration vs delivery vs deployment, the pipeline stages, build-once immutable artifacts, deployment strategies (rolling, blue-green, canary), rollback, and quality gates.

t: Caching — Cache-Aside, Eviction Policies & Redis vs Memcached
d: A practical guide to caching — cache-aside, write-through, eviction policies, Redis vs Memcached, and how to avoid cache stampede and inconsistency.

t: DNS — The Internet's Name System
d: A deep dive into DNS — how names resolve to IPs through recursive and authoritative servers, the hierarchy, record types, caching and TTL, GeoDNS and anycast for load balancing, and DNS reliability and security.

t: Database Indexing — B-Tree vs LSM-Tree
d: A deep dive into database indexing — why indexes exist, B-tree vs LSM-tree structures, clustered vs secondary indexes, composite and covering indexes, the leftmost-prefix rule, and when not to index.

t: Docker & Containers
d: A deep dive into Docker and containers — the dependency problem they solve, how namespaces and cgroups isolate processes, containers vs VMs, image layers, the Dockerfile, registries, and why containers won.

t: Elasticsearch — Search & Analytics Engine
d: A deep dive into Elasticsearch — the inverted index, analyzers and tokenization, BM25 relevance scoring, shards and replicas, the distributed scatter-gather query path, near-real-time search, and queries vs filters.

t: GraphQL vs REST Comparison — Schema, Resolvers & When to Use Each
d: A deep technical comparison between GraphQL and REST — resources, API design, resolvers, and when to use each.

t: Kubernetes (k8s) — Pods, Services & Cluster Architecture
d: A deep dive into Kubernetes — pods, services, ingress, deployments, StatefulSets, master/worker architecture, namespaces, and Helm.

t: Load Balancing — Algorithms, Layer 4 vs 7, Health Checks
d: A practical guide to load balancing — algorithms, layer 4 vs layer 7, health checks, sticky sessions, and common interview tradeoffs.

t: Message Queue — Delivery Semantics, DLQ & Kafka Comparison
d: A practical guide to message queues — why they exist, delivery semantics, pull vs push, dead-letter queues, and how they compare to Kafka.

t: NoSQL — Document, Key-Value, Column-Family & CAP Theorem
d: A practical guide to NoSQL databases — document, key-value, column-family, and graph stores, CAP theorem, and when to choose NoSQL over SQL.

t: OAuth 2.0 — Authorization Code, PKCE & Grant Types Explained
d: A practical guide to OAuth 2.0 — authorization grant types, roles, protocol flow, access tokens, and how OAuth differs from authentication.

t: Object Storage & S3
d: A deep dive into object storage (S3) — object vs block vs file storage, the flat namespace, the HTTP API and multipart/presigned uploads, durability via erasure coding, consistency, storage tiers, and scaling.

t: Observability — Metrics, Logs & Traces
d: A deep dive into observability — monitoring vs observability, the three pillars (metrics, logs, traces), Prometheus and OpenTelemetry, distributed tracing, SLI/SLO/SLA and error budgets, and alerting on symptoms.

t: Redis — In-Memory Data Structure Store
d: A deep dive into Redis — the in-memory data structure store: why it's single-threaded and fast, its data types, RDB vs AOF persistence, eviction, replication and Cluster, and the use cases that make it more than a cache.

t: Sharding — Consistent Hashing, Shard Keys & Database Partitioning
d: A practical guide to database sharding — horizontal vs vertical partitioning, sharding strategies, consistent hashing, hotspot avoidance, and cross-shard queries.

t: Spark Join Strategy — Broadcast, Sort-Merge, Shuffle Hash & Bucketing
d: A practical guide to Spark join strategies — broadcast join, shuffle hash join, sort-merge join, cartesian join, bucketing, and when to use each.

t: Tech Stacks Deep Dives — Kafka, Kubernetes, Caching & More — updoggtech
d: Tech stacks deep dives — Kafka, Kubernetes, load balancing, caching, sharding, NoSQL, OAuth, Spark, GraphQL vs REST.

t: Vector Databases — Embeddings & ANN Search
d: A deep dive into vector databases — embeddings, similarity metrics, approximate nearest neighbor (HNSW, IVF, quantization), what a vector DB adds over a library, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and hybrid search.

t: WebSockets & Realtime
d: A deep dive into WebSockets and real-time communication — why HTTP isn't enough, polling vs long-polling vs SSE vs WebSockets, the upgrade handshake, full-duplex connections, and scaling stateful connections with a pub/sub backplane.

t: gRPC & Protobuf
d: A deep dive into gRPC and Protocol Buffers — contract-first RPC, the protobuf binary format, HTTP/2 foundations, the four streaming call types, code generation, and gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL.

t: updoggtech — 系统设计、技术栈与 SDE 面试准备
d: updoggtech — 软件面试准备。面向 SDE 面试的系统设计、技术栈与 DDIA 笔记。

t: AI 编程 — 工具、Agent 与 LLM API
d: AI 编程深入——Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code、prompt engineering、AI agent、用 LLM API 构建、tool use 与 MCP,以及面试中的 AI。

t: AI 辅助编程:最佳实践与陷阱 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:AI 辅助编码的实践指南——审查每份 diff、维持理解、用 AI 写测试并自我验证、保持迭代小步、刻意的上下文管理、何时不该用 AI、secret/PII/合规、AI 技术债的“看起来对”陷阱、团队规范,以及诚实度量生产力。

t: 在编码面试中用 AI — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:在编码面试中使用 AI——三类面试形式(现场编码/带回家/系统设计)、公司政策差异、允许 vs 禁止决策表、AI 被允许时如何正确使用、监考与反作弊、用 AI 高效备考(题目生成/解法讲解/模拟面试/系统设计/STAR)、面试官在找什么、诚信线,以及不依赖 AI 的技能。

t: 用 Claude 与 OpenAI API 构建应用 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:用 Claude 与 OpenAI API 构建生产应用——Messages API 结构与角色、streaming、prompt caching、token 成本与估算、延迟优化、错误处理与指数退避、structured output(tool use)、API 密钥管理与服务端代理,以及如何选对模型。

t: Copilot vs Cursor vs Claude Code — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:GitHub Copilot、Cursor 与 Claude Code 对比——从自动补全到 agent 的自主性谱系、各自如何工作与读取上下文、典型工作流、同一任务三种做法、正面对比表、底层共享机制、定价/模型/隐私,以及如何按任务大小选对工具。

t: Harness 工程 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:Harness 工程——什么是 harness(模型 vs harness)、智能体循环、上下文组装(上下文工程 > 提示工程)、工具设计、记忆与压实、护栏/权限/沙箱、环境锚定的验证与自我纠正、错误恢复、评估与迭代、子智能体编排,以及为什么能力 ≈ 模型 × harness。

t: AI coding agent 是如何工作的 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:AI coding agent 的内部机制——agentic loop(plan/act/observe/repeat)、tool use 原语、ReAct 推理、上下文窗口管理与压缩、任务规划与 sub-agent、自我验证闭环,以及失败模式与护栏。

t: 面向代码的提示工程 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:面向代码生成与 agentic 任务的 prompt engineering——为什么 prompt 决定输出质量、给足上下文、用验收标准规约需求、先要计划、few-shot 示例、规约语言/风格/边界、精确迭代追问、测试驱动 prompting、分解大改动,以及常见反模式。

t: AI 时代的 SDE 面试 — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:AI 时代的 SDE 面试——样板坍缩、存活下来的技能(系统设计/调试/代码评审/需求澄清/算法直觉/权衡沟通)、AI 能大体替代什么、面试形式如何演进、把 AI 当学习放大器、耐久技能清单、按形式准备,以及用 AI 而不依赖它。

t: 工具使用、函数调用与 MCP — AI 编程
d: AI 编程:function calling(tool use)与 MCP 深入——机制与三阶段交互、用 JSON Schema 定义工具、tool-use 循环、并行 tool call、把 tool use 当 structured output、MCP 架构(Host/Client/Server)、三种原语、最小 MCP server、MCP vs. function calling、MCP vs. RAG,以及安全考量。

t: 联系与模拟面试
d: 预约面向 SDE 面试的 1:1 模拟面试或咨询。系统设计与编码面试准备。

t: DDIA 笔记 — 《设计数据密集型应用》
d: 《设计数据密集型应用》(DDIA)浓缩笔记——可靠性、数据模型、存储引擎等等。

t: DDIA 第1章 — 可靠、可扩展与可维护
d: DDIA 第 1 章:什么让系统可靠、如何用负载参数和百分位延迟推理可扩展性,以及可维护性的三大支柱(可运维性、简单性、可演化性)。

t: DDIA 第10章 — 批处理
d: DDIA 第 10 章:批处理——Unix 哲学与管道、MapReduce(map/shuffle/reduce)与分布式文件系统、reduce 侧/map 侧 join 与倾斜、schema-on-read 数据湖、不可变输入带来的容错,以及超越 MapReduce 的数据流引擎(Spark/Flink/Tez、血缘重算)。

t: DDIA 第11章 — 流处理
d: DDIA 第 11 章:流处理——事件流传输(队列 vs 基于日志的代理 Kafka)、数据库与流(CDC、事件溯源)、在流上计算(事件时间 vs 处理时间、窗口、stream/table join、checkpointing 与 exactly-once)。

t: DDIA 第12章 — 数据系统的未来
d: DDIA 第 12 章:数据系统的未来——数据集成(记录系统 + 派生数据、批与流统一)、解绑数据库(以事件日志为集成点、数据流派生函数、读/写路径)、追求正确性(端到端幂等、完整性 vs 及时性、审计),以及数据伦理。

t: DDIA 第2章 — 数据模型与查询语言
d: DDIA 第 2 章:关系、文档、图三大数据模型的取舍,阻抗失配与 N+1,读时/写时 schema,声明式 vs 命令式查询(SQL/MapReduce/Cypher/SPARQL)。

t: DDIA 第3章 — 存储与检索
d: DDIA 第 3 章:数据库如何存储和检索数据——LSM-tree(SSTable/memtable/布隆过滤器/compaction)vs B-tree、WAL、聚簇/非聚簇/覆盖索引、内存数据库、OLTP vs OLAP、数据仓库星型 schema 与列式存储/压缩/向量化。

t: DDIA 第4章 — 编码与演化
d: DDIA 第 4 章:数据编码与 schema 演化——向后/向前兼容、语言特定序列化的陷阱、JSON/XML、Protobuf/Thrift 字段标签、Avro 写者/读者 schema,以及通过数据库/服务(REST/RPC)/消息代理的三种数据流。

t: DDIA 第5章 — 复制
d: DDIA 第 5 章:复制的三种方法——单主/多主/无主、同步 vs 异步、故障转移陷阱、复制日志实现、复制滞后与读己之写/单调读/一致前缀读、多主写冲突、无主 quorum(w+r>n)与读修复/反熵。

t: DDIA 第6章 — 分区
d: DDIA 第 6 章:数据分区(分片)——键范围 vs 哈希分区、倾斜与热点、二级索引的本地(按文档)vs 全局(按 term)、再平衡(避开 hash mod N、固定/动态分区数),以及请求路由(路由层/分区感知客户端/ZooKeeper)。

t: DDIA 第7章 — 事务
d: DDIA 第 7 章:事务——ACID 的真正含义、弱隔离级别(read committed、snapshot isolation/MVCC)、丢失更新/写偏斜/幻读等竞态,以及可串行化的三种实现(串行执行、两阶段锁 2PL、可串行化快照隔离 SSI)。

t: DDIA 第8章 — 分布式系统的麻烦
d: DDIA 第 8 章:分布式系统的故障——部分失败、不可靠网络(超时、无界延迟)、不可靠时钟(time-of-day vs 单调、LWW 的危险、TrueTime)、进程暂停,以及 quorum、fencing token、Byzantine 故障与系统模型(安全性/活性)。

t: DDIA 第9章 — 一致性与共识
d: DDIA 第 9 章:线性一致性(vs 可串行化、CAP 代价)、排序与因果性(版本向量、Lamport 时间戳、全序广播)、分布式事务与共识(两阶段提交的 in-doubt 问题、容错共识 Paxos/Raft/Zab、FLP、ZooKeeper/etcd 协调服务)。

t: 系统设计面试题与拆解
d: 系统设计面试准备——电商、视频流、聊天应用、打车、URL 短链等等。

t: 设计聊天应用 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:聊天应用(WhatsApp/微信规模)——WebSocket 与 WebSocket Manager 路由、Snowflake 消息 ID、在线/离线投递与去重、写扇出 vs 读扇出、群聊有序、已读回执、在线状态心跳采样、媒体存储、扩展与容错、关键取舍。

t: 设计电商平台 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:电商平台——微服务 + API 网关、多语言持久化(SQL/NoSQL/Cassandra/Redis/ES)、结账管道与 Saga、订单状态机、库存与超卖、支付幂等、CDC 搜索、高峰扩展(缓存/分片)、可靠性与可观测性、安全与欺诈、退款与多区域、ML 推荐、关键取舍。

t: 设计 Google Drive / Dropbox — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:文件存储与同步(Google Drive/Dropbox)——分块、内容寻址去重、元数据平面 vs 块平面、同步算法、设备通知、冲突解决(冲突副本)、共享与权限、CDN 下载、持久性与容错、关键取舍。

t: 设计酒店预订应用 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:酒店预订(Booking.com/Expedia)——全程 SQL + ACID、DB 级 CHECK 约束防超卖、乐观锁并发控制、Redis TTL 结账持有、Kafka→Elasticsearch 搜索、预订状态机、支付授权/捕获与幂等、取消退款、可用性日历、分析、扩展容错、为什么不用 Cassandra、关键取舍。

t: 设计分布式键值存储 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:Dynamo 风格分布式键值存储——一致性哈希与虚拟节点、N 路复制与 preference list、quorum 可调一致性(W+R>N)、向量时钟冲突解决、hinted handoff、Merkle 树反熵、gossip 成员关系,以及 AP 取舍。

t: 设计实时排行榜 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:实时排行榜——为什么 SQL ORDER BY+COUNT 不扩展、Redis 有序集合(跳表)、ZADD/ZREVRANGE/ZREVRANK 操作、持久化与真相来源、top-k/排名/邻居、分片(按分数范围/按用户)、精确 vs 近似全局排名(分桶)、基于时间的榜、关键取舍。

t: 设计地图 Web 应用 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:地图平台(Google Maps)——容量估算、路网图数据模型、空间索引(Geohash/Quadtree/S2)、瓦片渲染与 CDN、路由算法(Dijkstra/A*/Contraction Hierarchies)、实时导航 WebSocket、位置跟踪与交通融合、搜索与地理编码、缓存/分片/容错、ML 分析、关键取舍。

t: 设计附近的人 / Yelp(邻近服务)— 系统设计
d: 系统设计:邻近/Yelp 服务——地理空间索引、为什么普通 B-tree 索引失败、Geohash 前缀、Quadtree 密度自适应、边界问题(8 个邻居)、数据模型、两步读路径、缓存与读扩展、按区域分片、排序、关键取舍。

t: 设计 Pastebin — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:Pastebin 文本分享——key 生成(随机 vs 计数器)、分离元数据与内容(DB 指针 + S3 blob、小粘贴内联)、读写路径、缓存与 CDN、过期清理、扩展与容错、安全与滥用防护、关键取舍。

t: 设计支付系统 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:支付系统——正确性优于可用性、幂等键(绝不双重扣款)、支付状态机与 webhook、复式记账账本、数据模型、exactly-once 效果、Saga(而非 2PC)、对账、PCI 合规与 tokenization、关键取舍。

t: 设计打车应用 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:打车应用(Uber/Lyft)——GPS 摄取管道(至多一次)、地理空间索引(Geohash/S2/QuadTree)、内存索引 + Cassandra、二分图匹配、行程状态机、ETA 与路由、动态定价、地图服务、数据模型、异步支付与幂等、多区域扩展与容错、关键取舍。

t: 设计 Ticketmaster / 抢票 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:Ticketmaster 抢票——无超卖的强一致库存、座位状态机与临时持有、原子并发控制(条件 UPDATE / FOR UPDATE / Redis 锁)、持有过期、虚拟候车室驯服尖峰、按一致性拆分路径(浏览 AP / 预订 CP)、支付 saga、热门事件热点、关键取舍。

t: 设计搜索自动补全 (Typeahead) — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:搜索自动补全(typeahead)——trie 前缀树、在每个节点预计算 top-k、按热度(时间衰减)排序、离线数据管道构建 trie、内存服务与按前缀分片、缓存与客户端防抖、实时 vs 批更新、关键取舍。

t: 设计一个 URL 短链 (TinyURL) — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:URL 短链(TinyURL)——需求与容量估算、API、短 ID 生成(哈希 vs Snowflake vs 范围分配)、布隆过滤器冲突检测、数据模型、读写路径与 Redis 缓存、301 vs 302、自定义别名、点击分析管道、过期清除、扩展与容错、限流与安全。

t: 设计视频流平台 — 系统设计
d: 系统设计:视频流平台(YouTube/Netflix)——TCP vs UDP vs QUIC、Kafka 上传管道、转码与 HLS/DASH ABR、Blob+Cassandra 存储、CDN 与签名 URL 播放授权、离线推荐、播放跟踪、转码扩展与容错、直播 vs VOD、关键取舍。

t: 技术栈深入 — Kafka、Kubernetes、缓存等
d: 技术栈深入——Kafka、Kubernetes、负载均衡、缓存、分片、NoSQL、OAuth、Spark、GraphQL vs REST。

t: API 网关与服务网格
d: 深入讲解 API 网关与服务网格——微服务中的横切关注点、南北向 vs 东西向流量、BFF 模式、sidecar 代理模型、mTLS,以及网关与网格的区别。

t: 缓存 (Caching)
d: 深入讲解缓存:分层缓存栈、五种读写策略(cache-aside/read-through/write-through/write-behind/write-around)、淘汰算法(LRU/LFU/ARC/FIFO/TTL)、TTL 设计、三大失效模式(击穿/穿透/雪崩)、热点 key、分布式缓存一致性、Redis vs Memcached、CDN 边缘缓存与可观测性。

t: CDN — 内容分发网络
d: 深入讲解 CDN——为什么边缘缓存能降低延迟、卸载源站,PoP 与 anycast 路由怎么工作,Cache-Control 与 TTL,推与拉,失效策略,以及动态加速。

t: CI/CD — 持续集成与交付
d: 深入讲解 CI/CD——持续集成 vs 持续交付 vs 持续部署、流水线各阶段、一次构建的不可变制品、部署策略(滚动、蓝绿、金丝雀)、回滚与质量门禁。

t: 数据库索引 — B-Tree 对比 LSM-Tree
d: 深入讲解数据库索引——为什么需要索引、B-Tree 与 LSM-Tree 两种结构、聚簇索引与二级索引、复合索引与覆盖索引、最左前缀规则,以及什么时候不该建索引。

t: DNS — 互联网的名字系统
d: 深入讲解 DNS——名字如何通过递归解析器和权威服务器解析成 IP、层级结构、记录类型、缓存与 TTL、用于负载均衡的 GeoDNS 与 anycast,以及 DNS 的可靠性与安全。

t: Docker 与容器
d: 深入讲解 Docker 与容器——它们解决的依赖问题、namespaces 与 cgroups 如何隔离进程、容器对比虚拟机、镜像分层、Dockerfile、registry,以及容器为什么胜出。

t: Elasticsearch — 搜索与分析引擎
d: 深入讲解 Elasticsearch——倒排索引、分词与分析器、BM25 相关性打分、分片与副本、分布式 scatter-gather 查询路径、近实时搜索,以及 query 与 filter 的区别。

t: GraphQL 与 REST 对比
d: 深入对比 GraphQL 与 REST:资源与 API 设计、过度/不足获取、解析器执行模型与 N+1 问题、DataLoader 批处理、缓存(APQ/cacheControl)、schema 设计、游标分页与 Relay Connection、错误处理与部分响应、订阅、联邦与 schema 拼接、查询复杂度安全,以及 REST vs GraphQL vs gRPC 决策矩阵。

t: gRPC 与 Protobuf
d: 深入讲解 gRPC 与 Protocol Buffers——契约优先的 RPC、protobuf 二进制格式、HTTP/2 基础、四种流式调用、代码生成,以及 gRPC 对比 REST 与 GraphQL。

t: Apache Kafka
d: 深入讲解 Apache Kafka——分布式事件流平台:发布/订阅模型、生产者/消费者、分区与副本、acks 与 ISR、消费者组与再均衡、日志压实、KRaft、性能调优、Kafka Streams/Connect 与 Schema Registry。

t: Kubernetes (k8s)
d: 深入讲解 Kubernetes(k8s)容器编排:Pod/Service/Ingress 等核心原语、Master/Worker 架构、YAML 配置、命名空间、Helm、调度与亲和性、网络(CNI/Service/Ingress)、存储(PV/PVC/StorageClass)、探针、自动扩缩(HPA/VPA/CA)、Operator 模式与资源管理。

t: 负载均衡 (Load Balancing)
d: 深入讲解负载均衡:L4 vs L7、轮询/最少连接/IP 哈希/一致性哈希等算法、健康检查、粘性会话、SSL/TLS 终止、DNS 负载均衡与 GeoDNS、Anycast、高可用与故障转移、服务网格,以及常见坑与最佳实践。

t: 消息队列 (Message Queue)
d: 深入讲解消息队列:队列解决的核心问题、队列语义 vs 提交日志语义、三种投递保证、有序与分区、推/拉消费模型、消费者组与竞争消费者、背压与流控、死信队列与毒丸消息、RabbitMQ vs SQS vs Kafka 全面对比,以及事务发件箱与 Saga 等可靠性模式。

t: NoSQL
d: 深入讲解 NoSQL:四大类(键值/文档/列族/图)及其适用场景、CAP 定理与 PACELC、一致性模型谱系、BASE vs ACID、数据建模(嵌入 vs 引用、面向查询建表)、DynamoDB/MongoDB/Cassandra/Neo4j 代表产品、何时不该用 NoSQL,以及分片与冲突解决。

t: OAuth 2.0
d: 深入讲解 OAuth 2.0 授权框架:关键角色、OAuth vs 认证(OIDC)的区别、四种授权类型(授权码/客户端凭证/设备授权/已弃用的隐式与密码)、刷新令牌、PKCE、JWT 访问令牌结构与校验、scope、state 与 CSRF 防护、常见安全坑与令牌防盗,以及真实用例。

t: 对象存储与 S3
d: 深入讲解对象存储(S3)——对象 vs 块 vs 文件存储、扁平命名空间、HTTP API 与分段/预签名上传、纠删码带来的持久性、一致性、存储分级与扩展。

t: 可观测性 — 指标、日志与追踪
d: 深入讲解可观测性——监控与可观测性的区别、三大支柱(指标、日志、追踪)、Prometheus 与 OpenTelemetry、分布式追踪、SLI/SLO/SLA 与错误预算,以及基于症状的告警。

t: Redis — 内存数据结构存储
d: 深入讲解 Redis——内存数据结构存储:为什么单线程却很快、它的数据类型、RDB 与 AOF 持久化、淘汰策略、复制与集群,以及让它不止是缓存的各种用途。

t: 分片 (Sharding)
d: 深入讲解数据库分片(Sharding):水平 vs 垂直分区、扩展阶梯、三种分片策略(哈希/范围/目录)、一致性哈希与虚拟节点、分片键设计、热点与重分片、跨分片查询与事务、分片 vs 复制,以及 Vitess/Cassandra/DynamoDB 等真实架构。

t: Spark Join 策略
d: 深入讲解 Apache Spark Join 策略:执行模型与 shuffle、为何 join 策略重要、广播 join、shuffle hash join、sort-merge join、笛卡尔 join、数据倾斜的检测与加盐、Skew Join Hint、Bucketing、Adaptive Query Execution (AQE)、读查询计划与调优参数,以及 join 决策树。

t: 向量数据库 — Embedding 与 ANN 搜索
d: 深入讲解向量数据库——embedding、相似度度量、近似最近邻(HNSW、IVF、量化)、向量库比库本身多了什么、检索增强生成(RAG)与混合搜索。

t: WebSockets 与实时
d: 深入讲解 WebSockets 与实时通信——为什么 HTTP 不够、轮询 vs 长轮询 vs SSE vs WebSockets、升级握手、全双工连接,以及用发布/订阅背板扩展有状态连接。
